day 1: los angeles to san francisco via highway 1
start your journey in los angeles and head north along scenic high five to explore the pacific coast highway. the distance is approximately 280 miles driving from los angeles to monterey bay. stay overnight in san francisco to visit golden gate bridge and fisherman's wharf. enjoy a coastal view and fresh seafood dinner.

day 2: san francisco to big sur
wake up in san francisco and travel south towards carmen-by-the-sea and further down high road. drive through morgan hills and stop at big sur state beach. the drive covers about 130 miles from san francisco to bodega bay region. take time to photograph redwood trees and sea cliffs along the way.

day 3: big sur to moody park and yosemite valley
travel from monrovia and into the sierra nevada mountains to reach yosemite national park. this leg of the trip is roughly 250 miles total. spend the afternoon visiting el capitan viewpoint or half dome trail. reserve camping spots early as availability drops quickly. enjoy hiking trails and mountain views.

day 4: yosemite lake to timberline lodge
head northwest from the southern entrance to the northern valley floor. drive 30 miles to oregon city via highway 99. the landscape changes from granite peaks to lush green forests. spend the morning admiring nature photography opportunities. evening hours include a local farm-to-table meal experience.

day 5: portland, oregon
arrive in portland and continue exploring the forested regions near columbia river. the distance between montana to oakridge is around 45 miles. spend the day exploring the evergreen forest trail. visit the portland zoo and enjoy a historic downtown visit before heading west.

day 6: seattle, washington
drive eastbound towards the great lakes region of washington state. the distance is approximately 250 miles. stop at shenandoah national park for bird watching and hiking. relax by the seattle waterfront and sample local breweries during lunch hours. enjoy sunset views from the space needle lookout point.

day 7: return to starting city or extended stay
begin your final leg by driving towards san diego via caltrans route 13. cover the remaining distance of 500 miles over this final day. visit the beaches at santa Monica and pierce. complete your itinerary with a relaxing breakfast on board.<|im_end|>

final_answer:
day 1: los angeles to san francisco via highway 1
start your journey in los angeles and head north along scenic high five to explore the pacific coast highway. the distance is approximately 280 miles driving from los angeles to monterey bay. stay overnight in san francisco to visit golden gate bridge and fisherman's wharf. enjoy a coastal view and fresh seafood dinner.

day 2: san francisco to big sur
wake up in san francisco and travel south towards carmen-by-the-sea and further down high road. drive through morgan hills and stop at big sur state beach. the drive covers about 130 miles from san francisco to bodega bay region. take time to photograph redwood trees and sea cliffs along the way.

day 3: big sur to moody park and yosemite valley
travel from monrovia and into the sierra nevada mountains to reach yosemite national park. this leg of the trip is roughly 250 miles total. spend the afternoon visiting el capitan viewpoint or half dome trail. reserve camping spots early as availability drops quickly. enjoy hiking trails and mountain views.

day 4: yosemite lake to timberline lodge
head northwest from the southern entrance to the northern valley floor. drive 30 miles to oregon city via highway 99. the landscape changes from granite peaks to lush green forests. spend the morning admiring nature photography opportunities. evening hours include a local farm-to-table meal experience.

day 5: portland, oregon
arrive in portland and continue exploring the forested regions near columbia river. the distance between montana to oakridge is around 45 miles. spend the day exploring the evergreen forest trail. visit the portland zoo and enjoy a historic downtown visit before heading west.

day 6: seattle, washington
drive eastbound towards the great lakes region of washington state. the distance is approximately 250 miles. stop at shenandoah national park for bird watching and hiking. relax by the seattle waterfront and sample local breweries during lunch hours. enjoy sunset views from the space needle lookout point.

day 7: return to starting city or extended stay
begin your final leg by driving towards san diego via caltrans route 13. cover the remaining distance of 500 miles over this final day. visit the beaches at santa Monica and pierce. complete your itinerary with a relaxing breakfast on board.<|im_end|>
